stories.title: Nolan Wells' cause and manner of death undetermined, attorney says, citing family's autopsy
stories.summary: Nolan Wells, an 18-year-old Mississippi teen, died under undetermined circumstances, according to preliminary findings from an independent autopsy. The cause and manner of death remain unknown, pending further investigation. Wells' body was found on July 6 on Horn Island, where he had been on a Fourth of July outing with friends. The investigation continues, with the official autopsy results still pending.

stories.title: Live updates: Trump attends dignified transfer of U.S. service members
stories.summary: Former President Trump attended a dignified transfer of four US service members killed in an Iranian attack on July 17 at the Dover Air Force Base in Delaware. The transfer involved the repatriation of the remains of the service members who died in the attack. Trump met with the families of the deceased service members during the ceremony. The US is still investigating the circumstances of the attack.

stories.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
stories.summary: Marvel's Wonder Man, Simon Williams, faces embezzlement and murder charges, marking a significant turn in his career. The charges are revealed in the latest issue of Wonder Man 5, set to hit stores on July 22nd. This development comes as a major setback for the character, who has been a prominent figure in the Marvel Cinematic Universe. The consequences of these charges will likely have a lasting impact on Wonder Man's future.

stories.title: Sestao River - Athletic Club: horario y dónde ver hoy en TV el partido amistoso de pretemporada
stories.summary: The Athletic Club will face Sestao River in an exhibition match at Las Llanas on Wednesday, July 22, at 19:30 hours. This match is part of the team's pre-season training, following a 0-11 victory over SD Leioa in their previous friendly. The game will be streamed live through the Athletic Club's official channel and platform. The match marks a step up in intensity for the team as they continue to build their physical and tactical progression.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina react to final defeat and Messi questions
stories.summary: Argentina's World Cup campaign ended in a 1-0 defeat to Spain in extra time on Sunday in New Jersey, with midfielder Enzo Fernandez being sent off for two yellow cards. Fernandez's statement on social media praised the team's commitment and humility, despite the loss. The match was marred by ugly scenes, including Fernandez's red card and dissent towards the referee. The team's performance will be scrutinized in the aftermath of the loss.

stories.title: Pittsburgh Pirates vs. New York Yankees: Mitch Keller vs. Gerrit Cole
stories.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates face the New York Yankees in the series finale at Yankee Stadium on July 22, with Mitch Keller making his first start since July 9. Keller aims to bounce back from a poor performance against the Atlanta Braves, while Gerrit Cole looks to secure his first win since July 3. The Pirates are seeking to end a two-week drought without Keller's start. The Yankees aim to win an interleague series for the third time in six tries.

stories.title: Why Jalen Brunson wasn't chosen as the NBA2K27 cover athlete over Victor Wembanyama
stories.summary: Victor Wembanyama has been chosen as the cover athlete for NBA2K27. The 19-year-old Spurs center was selected despite the Knicks' Jalen Brunson leading his team to an NBA championship and earning the NBA Finals MVP award. Brunson's recent postseason success did not secure him the cover spot. Wembanyama's selection will be featured in the upcoming video game.

stories.title: Wemby, Rose, Clark are NBA 2k27 Cover Athletes
stories.summary: NBA 2K27 cover athletes announced, featuring Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose. The trio will be on different editions of the game, available on September 4. Wembanyama, a 22-year-old NBA star, will be on the Standard Edition cover, following his 2026 NBA Finals appearance and Kia Defensive Player of the Year award. The game's release will mark the beginning of the NBA 2K27 season.

stories.title: The Guardian view on Andy Burnham and devolution: it takes a powerful centre to decentralise power | Editorial
stories.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham has signaled his intent to bolster the power of Downing Street, despite citing overcentralization of power as a cause of Britain's social and economic issues. This move comes as he aims to decentralize power, a goal that has been hindered by the Treasury's reluctance to grant fiscal control to lower tiers of government. The UK's devolved administrations have historically struggled with genuine control, often shifting blame to national government when things go wrong. The prime minister's office is also under-resourced compared to equivalent institutions in similar democracies.

stories.title: How Long Can ‘The Odyssey’ Stay on Imax 70mm Screens? Nolan Fans Scramble as New Showtimes Sell Out 7 Weeks in Advance
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" has sold out Imax 70mm screenings in New York and Los Angeles through August 19, and in London through September 10, due to intense demand. The film's premium large format showings are available in 25 US theaters and 41 globally. Imax has committed to "Dune: Part Three" for its next 70mm release, meaning "The Odyssey" will not have to vacate screens until then. The next Imax releases include "The End of Oak Street" and "Resident Evil," but it's unclear if they will also be shown in 70mm.

stories.title: Ex-Man Utd midfielder Casemiro joins Inter Miami
stories.summary: Former Manchester United midfielder Casemiro has signed with Inter Miami on a deal running to the end of the 2027 season, with an option to extend until June 2029. The 34-year-old Brazilian international joins the Major League Soccer side after making 160 appearances for United, scoring 26 goals and providing 14 assists. Casemiro's signing comes as United prioritize midfield reinforcements, acquiring Andrey Santos and Youri Tielemans. He is expected to bolster Inter Miami's roster ahead of the new season.

stories.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly developing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely feature 12GB of RAM, an upgrade from the original 8GB. The new model is expected to retain the same price point as the current Neo, but ongoing component shortages may impact availability. The updated Neo is also expected to be released alongside new iMacs with OLED screens. Apple has not confirmed the release date or pricing for the updated models.

stories.title: Sacha Baron Cohen’s ‘Ali G: Who Iz I?’ Sets October Release From Amazon MGM
stories.summary: Sacha Baron Cohen's "Ali G: Who Iz I?" is set to release in theaters on October 23. The film marks Cohen's feature directorial debut, in which he plays the character Ali G, interviewing people on the street and getting into humorous and cringe-worthy situations. Cohen originated the character in 1998 for Channel 4's "The 11 O'Clock Show" and reprised it in his own "Da Ali G Show" in the early 2000s. The movie will be available on the same date, with no further details on its distribution.

stories.title: Philipsen sprints to stage 17 win
stories.summary: Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France, crossing the finish line with a significant gap over his rivals. The 28-year-old Alpecin-Premier Tech rider secured his first stage victory in a three-week race marked by frustration. Philipsen's win came after a powerful lead-out from teammate Mathieu van der Poel on the 174.7km stage from Chambery to Voiron. He now looks to build momentum heading into the final stages of the Tour.

stories.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
stories.summary: Natalie Fleet has stepped down as safeguarding minister, citing the role as "overwhelmingly triggering" due to her own experiences as a victim of grooming and rape. She was appointed to the role on May 12 this year. Fleet says she cannot perform the job effectively and is offering her support to her successor. The new Prime Minister Andy Burnham has been informed of her departure.

stories.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
stories.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, with Welsh Fire reaching 118-8 in their chase of 126-7. The Brave's fightback was sparked by 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman, who dismissed Georgia Voll for 52. Lauren Bell and captain Sophie Molineux also contributed to the win with key wickets. The Brave will look to build on this momentum in their next match.

stories.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
stories.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and his crewmates are set to return to Earth after a 241-day mission aboard the International Space Station. The crew, consisting of Williams, Roscosmos cosmonauts Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev, will undock from the space station at 3:02 a.m. EDT on July 26. They will land in Kazakhstan at 6:26 a.m. EDT, completing 3,856 orbits and traveling over 102 million miles. The crew will then be flown to recovery teams in Karaganda, Kazakhstan.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of individual willpower. The report highlights a flaw in decades of British food policy, specifically the "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework, which unfairly targets fat as a primary driver of weight gain. The evidence suggests sugar and refined carbohydrates are the main culprits, not fat. The committee recommends targeting ultra-processed foods to reduce obesity in Britain.

stories.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
stories.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has unveiled a new image of the spiral galaxy Messier 94, also known as NGC 4736, on June 30, 2026. The galaxy features a bright inner ring where new stars are forming, fueled by gas driven in its unique oval-shaped structure. This layered image combines X-rays of different wavelengths with a visible light image from ground-based telescopes. The image is now accompanied by a unique sound experience.

stories.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
stories.summary: Benicio Del Toro is set to star in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody" alongside Ben Stiller, marking Del Toro's first lead series comedy role. The show follows a disgraced financier accused of massive fraud, navigating prison politics and consequences. Del Toro is a three-time Oscar nominee, including a win for best supporting actor in 2001 for "Traffic". The series is set to premiere on Apple TV.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns, and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants denied the charges and their lawyers requested extradition to Russia. The case is linked to Africa Politology, a Russian political network accused of spreading anti-Western narratives in Angola.

stories.title: Mamdani backs off on pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has backed off on his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of authority to do so. Mamdani had previously called for Netanyahu's arrest over alleged war crimes, but now says US officials must act on the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant. The US is not an ICC member state, making it unlikely Netanyahu will be arrested. Mamdani's reversal comes after President Donald Trump stated Netanyahu would not be arrested in the US.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The US government has terminated 53 out of 67 grants for a $100 million teen pregnancy prevention program, effective immediately, citing a lack of alignment with the administration's priorities. The program, which aimed to reduce teen pregnancies, will now focus on promoting marriage and starting families. The move follows a similar effort in 2018 that was blocked by courts. The program's remaining grants are expected to be re-evaluated in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Why Are Young People in India Protesting?
stories.summary: A youth-led movement angry over the education system and a lack of jobs is posing a rare challenge to a government that has ruled for more than a decade with a firm hand.

stories.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the stabbing death of 65-year-old Karen Carter, a British-South African mother of four, in the French village of Trémolat last year. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car in April 2023. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She is now formally charged following judicial investigations that began on May 7, 2025.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
stories.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
stories.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City's legionnaires' disease outbreak on Manhattan's Upper East Side, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers across 33 buildings, where live legionella bacteria were detected. The city's health department is continuing to test the remaining 140 cooling tower systems. Further cases may be confirmed as testing is completed.

This development comes as a major setback for the character, who has been a prominent figure in the Marvel Cinematic Universe. The consequences of these charges will likely have a lasting impact on Wonder Man's future.
